model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: SMU holds a commanding edge in every adjusted efficiency metric, ranked #42 nationally by both KenPom and Torvik with an AdjEM of +18.11 compared to Miami (OH)'s +8.25, a gap that is reflected in the 7.5-point betting line. Miami (OH)'s impressive 31-1 record is largely a product of the weak MAC schedule, and expert analysts noted their résumé may not have even been the best in their own conference. SMU's ACC battle-testing against elite competition gives them a meaningful preparation edge, and the key injury concern around star guard BJ Edwards was resolved with reports confirming his expected availability for the tournament. All major indicators — efficiency ratings, betting market, schedule strength, and expert analysis — point clearly to the Mustangs advancing.

reasoningStep.title: SMU vs. Miami (OH) First Four Midwest Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: SMU is a substantial 7.5-point favorite backed by a ~10-point AdjEM advantage over Miami (OH). The RedHawks' 31-1 record is heavily inflated by a weak MAC schedule, while SMU's ACC pedigree provides a meaningful quality-of-competition edge. BJ Edwards' injury concern was resolved pre-tournament, and all major indicators point to SMU advancing.
evidence: KenPom ranks SMU #42 (AdjEM +18.11, AdjOE #27, AdjDE #95) vs. Miami (OH) #93 (AdjEM +8.25, AdjOE #68, AdjDE #149) — a ~10-point efficiency gap favoring SMU
Torvik confirms the same rankings: SMU #42 vs. Miami OH #87, with SMU's offense ranked 26th nationally
Betting market has SMU as a 7.5-point favorite with an O/U of 163.5, reflecting strong consensus on SMU's superiority
BJ Edwards (SMU star) was injured late in the ACC Tournament but is expected to be available for the NCAA Tournament per MSN reporting
Jay Bilas noted Miami (OH)'s 31-1 record may not reflect true quality, suggesting Akron had a comparable résumé despite losing the head-to-head
SMU played in the ACC (nation's toughest conference) while Miami (OH) dominated the MAC, a significant schedule-strength gap

Mar. 14, 2026 at 1:59am Got story updates? Submit your updates here. › Following SMU's loss to Louisville in the ACC Tournament, the Mustangs are on the NCAA Tournament bubble. However, ACC commissioner Jim Phillips defended SMU's resume, citing the team's strong performance when star player BJ Edwards was healthy and the impact of his recent injury. Phillips noted SMU's nine wins against top opponents and lack of bad losses, arguing they deserve a spot in the Big Dance. Why it matters SMU's NCAA Tournament hopes are hanging in the balance, and the impact of BJ Edwards' injury could be the deciding factor. As an experienced member of the NCAA selection committees, Phillips' public defense of SMU's resume carries significant weight and could sway the committee's decision. The details SMU went 20-13 overall this season and 8-10 in conference play. When BJ Edwards, who averages 12.7 points, 5.9 rebounds, 4.9 assists and 2.3 steals, was in the lineup, the Mustangs were 19-9. However, Edwards missed the last five games due to injury, and SMU went just 1-4 during that stretch. Phillips argued that SMU's nine wins against Quadrant 1 and Quadrant 2 opponents, including four in Q1, as well as their lack of bad losses, make them deserving of an NCAA Tournament bid. SMU lost to Louisville in the ACC Tournament on March 13, 2026. BJ Edwards suffered his injury and missed the last five games of the regular season. You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.

The backlash was swift and unrelenting. I was criticized for not picking an underdog to reach San Antonio. But when the dust cleared, all four No. 1 seeds reached the Final Four , and the Cougars and Gators battled it out for the title. You're welcome -- and I forgive you. All of you. This season is different. Cinderella just took a year off. The top teams aren't so much better than the rest of the field, so there will be more surprises. Mar. 14, 2026 at 1:59am Got story updates? Submit your updates here. › Following SMU's loss to Louisville in the ACC Tournament, the Mustangs are on the NCAA Tournament bubble. However, ACC commissioner Jim Phillips defended SMU's resume, citing the team's strong performance when star player BJ Edwards was healthy and the impact of his recent injury. Phillips noted SMU's nine wins against top opponents and lack of bad losses, arguing they deserve a spot in the Big Dance. Why it matters SMU's NCAA Tournament hopes are hanging in the balance, and the impact of BJ Edwards' injury could be the deciding factor. As an experienced member of the NCAA selection committees, Phillips' public defense of SMU's resume carries significant weight and could sway the committee's decision. The details SMU went 20-13 overall this season and 8-10 in conference play. When BJ Edwards, who averages 12.7 points, 5.9 rebounds, 4.9 assists and 2.3 steals, was in the lineup, the Mustangs were 19-9. However, Edwards missed the last five games due to injury, and SMU went just 1-4 during that stretch. Phillips argued that SMU's nine wins against Quadrant 1 and Quadrant 2 opponents, including four in Q1, as well as their lack of bad losses, make them deserving of an NCAA Tournament bid. SMU lost to Louisville in the ACC Tournament on March 13, 2026. BJ Edwards suffered his injury and missed the last five games of the regular season.
